items.prompt: identifies bounded input-output relationships as functions or non-functions across tables, mappings, and graphs
items.expected_evidence: identify inputs and outputs in bounded function situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies function structure and variable roles correctly.

items.prompt: explains why a relation is or is not a function using the one-input-to-one-output condition
items.expected_evidence: explain the one-input-to-one-output condition in age-appropriate language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains function status through coherent input-output reasoning.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to representation_function_disconnect.
items.expected_evidence: connect verbal rules, tables, points, and simple symbolic forms as representations of the same function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer lost the function relationship across representations.

items.prompt: succeeds across symbolic, table, verbal, graph, and simple contextual function representations
items.expected_evidence: determine whether a table, mapping, or graph does or does not represent a function
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves function meaning across varied representations and contexts.
